rationale: This is a clean removal of a self-contained visual system. The microGeometry module has well-defined integration points (quality config, compound modes, mode rotation, system registry) with no deep coupling to other systems. All references are accounted for in the plan. Removing it reduces visual mode variety slightly but aligns with the stated artistic direction of preferring point-based and line-based rendering over instanced mesh primitives.

dependency_analysis.notes: No other system imports from microGeometry.ts. The crystal+microgeometry compound mode is the only cross-system coupling and it is correctly targeted for removal.

risk_assessment.mitigation_notes: The plan's verification step (step 11) with grep sweeps for orphaned references is sufficient mitigation. The mode count reduction from 8 single + 4 compound to 7 single + 3 compound is a minor visual variety change, not a stability risk.

test_files: tests/visual/modeManager.test.ts (remove microgeometry entries from 4 test arrays)
tests/visual/compoundModes.test.ts (remove microgeometry refs, update count assertions)
tests/visual/quality.test.ts (remove 2 maxInstances tests, remove 2 scaling assertions)

verdict: Plan is accurate and complete. All file references and line numbers verified against codebase. No missing targets or orphaned references detected. Safe to execute.
